channel.title: Comments for page "Funerals Are Fun"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Funerals Are Fun"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Call me weird but, i love seeing entities that think they have all the power and nothing can stop them, only to be stopped by something hilarious that's completely out of their control and they don't understand how or why its so powerful and why things aren't going the way they want it to.

I imagine this is the first time probably since the first ever funeral that cousin johnny was interrupted and not had his way. And it was not from some ancient elder god or world destroying reality bender, it was from a game show host, and it's glorious. +1

it reminds me of one of my favourite tales on the site, Tricks, a sort of spiritual successor to Treats. !SPOILERS! SCP-106 ends up being completely destroyed and recontained by SCP-2006 playing around and telling him that he's old and not scary.

item.title: Huh...
item.content:encoded: I think this was actually the first of the SCP "Tales" I read, but have only just really signed up for the site itself. I did want to say that I really like this. Always love crossing over different horrors and seeing what comes about from them. So as a concept, I love it.
Thank you so much, everyone! My spleen graciously accepts this honor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cousin Johnny literally turned into the physical embodiment of the "I came here to have a good time and I'm honestly feeling so attacked right now" meme and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Readings and audio adaptations are always welcome! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a day late and a dollar short, but I done did a reading!

item.title: ...Christ
item.content:encoded: This is one of the best tales on the site. The disgusting horror from the Skips, the comical dissonance from the family member, Cousin Johney getting upstaged, the eulogy, Laughy's speech, the ending line, it's practically perfection!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this simultaneously captures the body horror of Series I and the experimentalism of Series III. It's scary, but also strangely amusing and avant garde.

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: This is awesome, and thank you!

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I love getting requests from the authors! I hope ya'll enjoy this reading!

https://youtu.be/sW2g_bP_Vt4

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it too cheesy for me to say I did not see that coming?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is easily one of my favorite tales, and part of that comes from the absurd humor. It's hard to be scared of LAUGH IS FUN or Cousin Johnny when they're just so damn hilarious!

Though now that I look at that comment, it occurs to me that there's either something wrong with me or I'm just stating the obvious about the tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! This was definitely fun to write and put together. And yeah, the eulogy took some doing: had to be word salad, but it couldn't be Reagan-style. Less doom and gloom, more joshing about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this. The eulogy was especially funny and unsettling.

Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's a combo I never expected. I'm a fan, nice work. It's a funny kind of surreal.
+1 :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well said, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, it goes deeper than that.

Cousin Johnny is family. He's the well-worn comfort of the home. He wants to make you happy, remind yourself what the holiday spirit is all about. He's all about good times and reminiscing and partaking of each other and getting people comfortable with using that bond of blood between them.

Laughy McLaugherson is the entertainment industry. He's the commercialization of the holiday spirit, the presentation of big-budget special effects with an interest in making everyone happy. Laughy is the stranger, some say the hero, some say the ruiner of family ties as everyone stops thinking of each other and watches him, instead.

If Cousin Johnny is Thanksgiving, Laughy McLaugherson is Black Friday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really think is interesting, not just because my dad's in it, but because this tale is, at its core, two different humanoid scips that make people feel so happy despite disgusting body horror happening around then. And that, I think, is what this holiday season is all about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is what I love. Because of how 2030 operates, all these people are gonna disappear and die soon anyway. Just that, you know, here they won't die at Cousin Johnny's hands.

Hmm, this is rather reminiscent of the Fear mythos (where humans are plagued by eldritch manifestations of various concepts of Fear, and the only way to escape from one of them is to die by another Fear).

But it's all fair. After all, if you avoid death in a certain manner, death will still find you… eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. They are really weird animals. Too bad they probably won't be around much longer. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Except that it's not specifically injected, which means that it isn't, strictly speaking, a venom.

This is unfortunately one case where the biology isn't well defined by the nomenclature.

ETA: Also, I just got compared to an ophite skip. I'm damn proud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are both venomous as well as poisonous (although the venom/poison gland is actually on the arm; which is weird). Presumably Ross died by venom (i.e. a slow loris bit him) rather than poison (he bit a slow loris). Though either is possible, they lick the gland while grooming, which spreads poison over the fur and they retain the saliva as venom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Slow Loris Facts
- No Loris Facts

This is a slow loris.

Yes, they are poisonous, and people have died from their bites. Ross was actually killed by a slow loris.

Story-wise, I wanted a rash of bizarre fatal phenomena nearby as a bit of foreshadowing that LAUGH IS FUN was filming in the area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, this was beautiful nonsense and I loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is canon now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HOW COULD YOU WRITE THIS AND NOT TELL ME

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You effectively turn SCP-2030 into a sort of hero here. A weird, creepy, disgusting, homicidal, hilarious hero.

This is what I love. Because of how 2030 operates, all these people are gonna disappear and die soon anyway. Just that, you know, here they won't die at Cousin Johnny's hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This is sooooo damn good.

+ Show Praise
- Hide

I'm not sure if I'm just a sucker for this style of writing, but I just love the "cognitive dissonance"/aphasiatic tone here and I think you do it so well. It really feels like every word was perfectly chosen. I put this in the same category as SCP-1877 which, believe me, is high praise from me.

We were wondering how we were going to finish bleeding for Ross without the knife when the rest of us started developing pains in our left side.
…
He just started prematurely regurgitating his blood and wood pulp into the casket…

I just really love the word choice here. You show how our bystander has anomalously learned the ritual, even to the point where he and the family are thrown off when it doesn't go according to plan.

He reached over to the tabernacle and opened it up to reveal a hidden camera! Then, he went over to Aunt Maureen, stuck a finger up her nose and pulled out another camera! Oh my god, we were on a prank show all this time!

You effectively turn SCP-2030 into a sort of hero here. A weird, creepy, disgusting, homicidal, hilarious hero.

This was way over the top, and you know Ross would have wanted it this way.

Also great word choice here, with the bystander just getting soooo into it!

The announcer's final note was great too, you captured that alien/humorous/confused/creepy tone perfectly.

I had forgotten how fun funerals are. We should have them more often.

And you even managed to end by showing that our protagonist is still affected by the homicidal influences of SCP-2852, maybe even moreso than he would have otherwise been.

My brother Ross died a week ago in a freak accident involving a slow loris. Turns out, they're related to monkeys, and poisonous. Who knew? I never knew Ross was into slow lorises, anyway. The doctor said there was a rash of slow loris attacks recently, which is strange, because they're kinda like sloths otherwise.

This is the one confusing thing about the whole skip. Is there something about the Slow Loris' I'm not getting? Or was he actually killed by a Slow Loris?

Great job! Very impressive, and much fun to read (and re-read).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's the idea. Black-Level Events can be disrupted, if you join the funeral and upstage Cousin Johnny to the point he ragequits.

…It pretty much requires SCP-2030 levels of upstaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, yes, I did write the 2123 entry!

I feel like, in the end, they probably came from the same place in my head. A maniacally dark, hilarious place. It's like a train wreck. Except the trains are clowns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a great way to bring a funeral to a close. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HOWDIDYOUMAKETHISSOPERFECTOMG

+1ONEHUNDREDANDONTEYONETHOUSANDAND1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the perfect response to Dexanote's challenge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Did 2030 just stop a Black Event from having any fall-out other than the people present AT the event?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As LAUGH IS FUN is possibly my favorite skip, I really wanted to upvote this. That said, I really think you should go back and add some more polish to this. As it is, it feels a teensy bit rushed.

I will upvote, because the concept and presentation are amazing and passable, respectively, but I urge you to return soon and really make this shine.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should feel proud for showing the world such over-the-top SCP crossovers. I am pretty sure that the SCP-2123 entry in SCP-2305-A Extended Documentation Log is also yours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved every second of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are no words to describe how fucking awesome and disturbing this is.

Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Written in response to Dexanote's challenge. I blame my cough medication.

Skips fighting are SCP-2852 vs. SCP-2030.
